articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]This sticky, adhesive fluid ignites when exposed to air. [br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]As an action, you can throw this flask up to 20 feet, shattering it on impact. [p]Make a ranged attack against a creature or object, treating the alchemist's fire as an improvised weapon. On a hit, the target takes 1d4 fire damage at the start of each of its turns. [p]A creature can end this damage by using its action to make a DC 10 Dexterity check to extinguish the flames.[br][br]

articles.title: Bracelet of the Wolf
articles.content:
The bracelet consists of 5 moonstones set in platinum, joined by a fine platinum chain. The bracelet is very strong desipte its fine craftsmanship.

[b]Rarity:[/b] Unique / Rare

[b]Identify:[/b] Requires 5th level caster.

[b]Symbolism:[/b] The stones represent 5 phases of the moon (waxing cresecnt, 1st half, full moon, last half, waning crescent) while the clasp, without a stone, represents the new moon. This, or something similar, is given to a werewolf pack's Luna as a symbol of her rank.

[b]Abilities[/b] (when used by a Luna):
[ul]
[li][b]Forced Transition:[/b] Force a member of the Luna's pack to change to/from wolf form. This can be done 3x / day, resetting at dawn. 

[li][b]Hybrid Form:[/b] Allows the Luna to attain and hold a form consisting of the head and claws of her wolf but maintain her humanoid body. This form is often more dangerous than the wolf form. (1x per day)

[li][b]Moonbeam:[/b] Ability to cast the Druid [i]Moonbeam[/i] spell as if the Luna was a 5th level Druid. This can be done 3x / day, resetting at dawn.
articles.history:
[ul]
  [li]Lost by the pack during a bloody coup attempt that resulted in the alpha and mate being killed
  [ul]
    [li]Also lost the alpha's symbol - a matching torc
  [/ul]
  [li]New leadersip is not well supported and considered illegitimate without the symbols of power
  [li]@[Karilinia Inturisdan](person:f4f671ed-3c46-47d1-aa37-4b13ca12d3c9) obtained if from a "dealer" that didn't know its true value
  [li]She was holding it for eventual new leadership
  [li][u]Karilinia's realtionship to the pack is TBD[/u]
  [li]@[Arada Thornkin](person:d839ea9d-4fb5-4f0c-8ba3-60a0d01b59e2) stole it from Karilinia
  [li]Karilinia doesn't mind that Arada has it since she 
    [ul]
      [li]can honsetly say she doesn't have it any more
      [li]trusts Arada not to lose it
      [li]will get it back eventually - one way or another
    [/ul]
[/ul]
articles.significance:
[ul]
  [li]Symbol of power for a clan of werewolves
  [li]Generally given to the pack's Luna (normally the alpha's mate)
[/ul]

articles.title: Doppel's Clone
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A steaming, fizzing, thin liquid that is yellow with brown accretions on the bottom. It smells and tastes like cinnamon.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]When Imbibed, the imbiber falls into a comatose trance, and an exact duplicate forms - complete with all abilities and memories of the original and with the accepted knowledge that it has a life span of just ten minutes. The duplicate can then perform the dangerous task without risk to the imbiber. [p]When ten minutes pass or the clone is destroyed, whichever comes first, the clone vanishes, and the imbiber wakes with full knowledge of the clone's actions. [p]The potion is imperfect, and there is a 10% chance the duplicate will go on a murderous rampage during its ten-minute lifespan.[br][br]

A fuse protrudes from the top of the cask. The cask weighs 20 lbs.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Once lit, the fuse burns quickly. 1d4 + 1 rounds after you ignite the fuse, the cask explodes. articles.title: Ebony Whistle of Taming
articles.content:
The Ebony Whistle of Taming has been created by a renowned beastmaster, Erind Wen Beermaster. He sought a way to connect with beasts on a level of mutual respect and understanding. After years of research and experimentation, he infused this whistle with a powerful enchantment, using rare materials and ancient rituals. The whistle has since passed through the hands of various adventurers, each using its power to forge alliances with the creatures of the wild.

[b]Item Type:[/b] Wondrous item, rare (requires attunement)

[b]Use/Activation:[/b] As an action, the user can blow the whistle while maintaining eye contact with a beast within 30 feet. The beast must be able to see the user and have an Intelligence of 4 or lower. The beast becomes tamed and friendly to the user, obeying their commands to the best of its ability.

[b]Duration:[/b] The effect lasts for d6 hours. Once the effect ends, the creature remembers its time under the whistle's influence but does not necessarily become hostile unless provoked.

[b]Charges:[/b] The whistle can be used once per day. It regains all expended charges at dawn.

[b]Special:[/b] If the whistle is used on a beast that already has an owner or has been tamed by another, the user must make a Charisma (Animal Handling) check contested by the original owner's Charisma (Animal Handling) check. The higher result wins the beast's loyalty.

[b]Notes:[/b] If the beast is forced to act against its nature or self-preservation instincts, it may make a Wisdom saving throw of DC 15 to break free from the whistle's influence.

articles.title: Emetic Cleanse
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A bubbling, oily draught that is yellow with gray bubbles. It smells like wax, but tastes like raspberries.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]When drunk, the potion causes the imbiber's entire body to shake uncontrollably for several seconds. It purges the poison from all bodily tissues and forms them into a three-inch-long rubbery capsule that is then vomited. [p]The purge removes all effects of the poison (except any damage already taken before imbibing), and the poison can be extracted from the capsule to be reused, but its potency is reduced by half. [p]The imbiber takes no damage from the potion but incapacitates them for two rounds while it does its work.[br][br]

Gunpowder is stored in small wooden kegs and water resistant powder horns. [br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Setting fire to a container full of gunpowder can cause it to explode, dealing fire damage to creatures within 10 feet of it (3d6 for a powder horn, 7d6 for a keg). [p]Setting fire to an ounce of gunpowder causes it to flare for 1 round, shedding bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet.[br][br]

articles.title: Morning Star
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]An orb about 4 inches in diameter, made of metal or some other hard material. It has several small holes that allow the vapors from it's contents to escape.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]As an action, you can throw this grenade at a point up to 30 feet away. At the start of your next turn, it emits an opaque cloud of poisonous gas filling a 20-foot radius sphere. Each creature that is completely within the cloud at the start of its turn must make a Constitution saving throw (DC 12) or become poisoned for 1 minute. A creature can make the saving throw again at the start of each of its turns, ending the poisoned condition on a success. [p]Additionally, the gas ignites violently on contact with an open flame, and each creature in the area must make a DC 15 Dexterity saving throw. A target takes 4d6 fire damage on a failed save, or half as much on a successful one. The gas dissipates naturally over a period of up to 1 minute, depending on the wind conditions.[br][br]

articles.title: Pendant of the Sisters of the Unicorn
articles.content:
While the details and size may vary, these items are all obsidian pendants that depict a unicorn in ivory. Richer members of the sisterhood will generally have spent more on their pendant's creation while others will posess one more simple. No matter the provenance, they will all be blessed by the local Mother, which ibues the item with its magical ability. 

[h2]Magical Ability[/h2]
The pendant glows red if the one holding/wearing it is not sexually chaste.
[br][br]
articles.sidebarcontent:
[img:5734357]
One of the pair of pendants commissioned and worn by @[Adrienne Geronde](person:fe12391b-39d9-4a0d-901e-dfbb25ccb9ef) and @[Marita Maggia](person:7c9dab1a-8221-41ca-83f6-0d3b49331e37).
articles.significance: Usually only worn by members of the Sisters of the Unicorn. More specifically, ones who are sexually chaste.

articles.title: Potion of Magic Mischief
articles.content:
[h2]Description[/h2]This potion is contained in a blue-green glass vial with embossed vines and leaves. A label and a tag state the potion’s name in crooked handwriting. [br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]This potion enables you to impersonate one characteristic of another person. When you drink the potion, you can target one humanoid within 30 feet that you can see and choose one of these effects: [p]• You can imitate the creature’s voice.[p]• You can copy the creature’s handwriting (you need to have a piece of paper with their writing to do so).[p]• You can mimic their appearance and mannerisms.[p][p]Awkward Complications.[p] A creature that consumes this potion suffers one random side effect from the Awkward Complications List. Roll 1d12 to find out which side effect affects the creature. The side effect lasts 10 minutes unless otherwise noted.
d12Result
1.When you try to speak, you meow instead.
2.You sneeze violently if you lie.
3.You can see 120 feet into the Ethereal Plane. If there is a ghost within this range, it immediately becomes aware of you.
4.Occasional hiccups transform parts of your face to animal counterparts, for example, a rabbit’s nose, a cat’s eyes, or a shark’s teeth. The DM determines the transformations.
5.You have no shadow or reflection for 24 hours.
6.Roll 1d20. On a roll of 1–18, all non-living items you touch turn into lead. On a roll of 19– 20, all non-living items you touch turn into gold. Only items that weigh less than 1 lb. are affected. This ability lasts 1 minute. Transformed items revert to their original substance after 24 hours.
7.A pie appears in the air 5 feet from you and flies towards one random creature’s face within 120 feet, using your ranged attack bonus.
8.You can speak to plants for 1d4 days as if you were affected by the spell speak with plants. During this time, you are unable to communicate verbally with other creatures.
9.One random non-living item within 30 feet of you levitates 10 feet upwards and hovers there for 1d6 hours. During this time, the item can’t be moved from its position.
10.One of your eyes changes colour and appearance. It may turn into the eye of a reptile, cat, goat, or an entirely different creature determined by the DM.
11.One piece of your footwear sticks to the floor or ground and can’t be removed from it.
12.An illusory duplicate of you materialises 10 feet away from you. During this time, it follows you and argues vehemently that it is the real version of you.
[br][br]

Any non-magical iron-based object (other than on the imbiber) that enters the aura immediately begins rusting. If not treated immediately, it will rust completely away in 1d4+2 rounds. [p]Each round an item is affected, it loses one level of effectiveness. For example, a sword would become a -1 sword after one round, and plate armor would drop to AC 17 after the first round, etc. [br][br]

articles.title: Potion of Steel
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A watery fluid that is cyan with purple globules. It smells like blueberries and cinnamon, but tastes like celery and grass.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Turns the imbiber’s skin as hard as steel, effectively turning the skin into the equivalent of full plate armor with an AC of 18. While in this state, the imbiber’s speed is reduced to 10 feet per round, and dashing is impossible.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 30 minutes[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Protection[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.history: An ancient sect created this potion to create an army of almost invulnerable soldiers.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of Sure Tracking
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A thick tonic that is yellow in color. It smells like beer and milk, but tastes like celery.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Makes it possible to unerringly track a single well-defined target. [br][br]

articles.history: Commissioned by the great historical bounty hunter Coyote Maverick as an aid to tracking.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of Tarrasque Constitution
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A glowing, slimy potion that is indigo in color. It smells and tastes like chemicals.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]When you drink this potion, your Constitution score becomes 30. The potion has no effect on you if your Constitution is equal to or greater than that score.[br][br]

articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A metallic draught that is red in color. It smells and tastes like blueberries.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]When you drink this potion, your Strength score becomes 30. The potion has no effect on you if your Strength is equal to or greater than that score.[br][br]

articles.title: Potion of the 8-Ball
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A bubbling, thick potion that is copper with yellow globules. It smells like carrots, but tastes like urine.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Allows the imbiber to calculate angles when working with a slingshot. [p]If an attack is successful, the ammunition can bounce to another target within 10’. If it hits, it can bounce again and keep repeating this action until an attack misses or there are no other creatures within 10’. [p]Note: This cascade of attacks can strike targets more than once. The attacker makes an attack roll for each bounce and rolls damage separately for each successful strike. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 attack[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Mechanic[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essences:[/b] Air, Fire, Shadow[br]
articles.history: A ranger who preferred hunting with a slingshot developed a unique potion that could cause him to aim so precisely and calculate angles so perfectly that he could strike multiple targets with one piece of ammunition.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Chameleon
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A chunky elixir that is obsidian in color. It smells and tastes like rot so it's a good thing you don't drink it![br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Covers the imbiber in a chameleonlike aura that extends to all clothing and gear worn by them. The imbiber blends perfectly into the environment for four hours, effectively rendering them invisible. However, the imbiber can still be discovered by sounds made or unnatural movement of vegetation as the imbiber travels through heavy vegetation. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Coat[p][b]Duration:[/b] 4 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Protection[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Shadow, Water[br][b]Notes:[/b] Chameleon skin[br]
articles.history: Created by the legendary tracker, Alson Leunis, to better hide while stalking his prey.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Dreaming Sage
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A glowing potion that is indigo in color. It smells and tastes like lemons.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]If the imbiber rolls a DC 15 Wisdom check, they gain a valuable clue that may help them in the next 24 hours.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 24 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Mind[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Life, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: A hedge witch discovered that a brew of certain herbs and mushrooms would take her on a mystica l journey that seemed to last days - but really lasted a single minute. In the journey, she would always gain insight she could bringback to the conscious world. People now use the potion to seek clues to their future and to help clarify difficult decisions. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Drunken Sailor
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A slimy liquid that is gray in color. It smells and tastes like butter.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Allows one to feel the full effects of alcohol and return to full sobriety once a situation arises that calls for the imbiber’s focused atiention. Once the task is complete, the pleasant drunk feeling returns. [p]The potion lasts until the body has fully metabolized the alcohol.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] Special[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Utility[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 5+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 19[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 5 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Fire, Life, Water[br]
articles.history: A savvy pirate captain stumbled upon the secret of this special concoction in a book collected from a wayward merchant ship. Since then, he mixed dribbles of potion into the rum barrels to keep his crew both happy and functional. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Elemental - Air
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A swirling, bubbling, viscious liquid whose color indicates the type of elemental, in this case clear. [p]It has a smell of a storm and drinking it feels like drinking from a jet of air.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Turns the imbiber into an elemental for one minute - complete with all the elemental's abilities. If the imbiber falls unconscious, they revert to their original form. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 minute[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Special[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Alkahest[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Air, Earth, Fire, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: This potion was designed by a powerful sorcerer who felt that elemental creatures held secrets that he could learn if only he could assume their form. [p][/p]

articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A swirling, bubbling, viscious liquid whose color indicates the type of elemental: in this case brown. If the imbiber falls unconscious, they revert to their original form. [br][br]

articles.title: Potion of the Glib Tongue
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A swirling, watery potion that is emerald in color. It smells like candy, but tastes like lemon and peppermint.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Gives confidence in social situations, loosening the tongue and forcing the imbiber to unleash a constant stream of small talk on various subjects. Those the imbiber talks with will come away with a good impression of the person but no clue what was discussed.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 2 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Skill[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 5+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 19[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 5 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Lithium Grease[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Air, Fire, Life[br]
articles.history: A minor noble with social anxiety had the keep's alchemist devise a potion to let him function more effectively in society. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Green Thumb
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A murky, viscious fluid that is brown in color. It smells like cabbage and mead, but tastes like red pepper.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Transfers vital energy into any plants touched, ensuring they grow successfully. The downside is that using the potion causes two levels of exhaustion once its effect ends. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 8 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Skill[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 5+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 19[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 5 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Life, Water[br]
articles.history: The hedge witch Agatha Lodris was a very poor gardener, making it hard for her to keep stocked with the ingredients she needed to perform her duties to the community. To alleviate the problem, she invested over two years in developing a means of improving her gardening skills. The end result was this potion that gave her a green thumb.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Hyena
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A yellow elixir. It smells like bread, but tastes like cinnamon.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Causes fits of uncontrollable, highly contagious, laughter. Every round the imbiber fails a DC 14 Wisdom check, everyone within 20’ of that person must also make a save or burst out laughing for 1d3 rounds. No other actions can be taken when afflicted with laughter.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 10 minutes[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Special[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Air, Fire, Life[br][b]Notes:[/b] Hyena liver[br]
articles.history: Created by a bard to enhance his comedic performances[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Iron Cage
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A reflective, oily potion that is rose in color. It smells and tastes like carrots and spinach.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Your mind becomes impenetrable to any psychic intrusion - even psychic attacks. When the potion wears off, the imbiber develops a terrible migraine headache that incapcitates them. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 24 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Protection[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 17+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 25[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 25 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Alkahest[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Fire, Life, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: After too many deals were stolen from under her, a powerful merchant commissioned a potion to be created that would keep her mind in an iron cage - immune to the effects of mind-reading creatures and powers. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Iron Fist
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A steaming, thick fluid that is vermillion with scarlet bubbles. It smells like licorice and coconut, but tastes like wine and celery.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]The hands become even deadlier weapons, gaining a bonus of 1d10 bludgeoning damage to each successful hand strike or punch.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 minute[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Mechanic[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 5+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 19[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 5 weeks[br][b]Other Notes:[/b] An iron hammer[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Distilled Alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Shadow, Water[br][b]Notes:[/b] An iron hammer[br]
articles.history: Created by the monk Jurok, this potion makes the hands iron hard. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Iron Stomach
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A smoking, chunky elixir that is amethyst with yellow glows. It smells like vanilla and ginger, but tastes like ashes and garlic.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Staves off the ill effects caused by any food for 24 hours. [br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 24 hours[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Protection[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 11+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 22[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 12.5 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Air, Fire, Life, Water[br]
articles.history:
Created for the diplomatc corps by an enterprising cleric The potion allowed the diplomats to eat whatever was served as delicacies in the far-flung lands they visited without offending their hosts. It also worked as protection against poisoned dishes.

It is still used today as a remedy for a delicate stomach or for festivals where overeating is the standard.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Jumping Spider
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A smoking, reflective, thick mixture that is gray in color. It smells like cabbage, but tastes like cream.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]The potion causes the imbiber to jump 20’ in a random direction every time they fail a DC 14 Constitution save. [p]If the jump puts them in danger, such as hiting a wall, falling through a window, or other life-threatening situations, the potion mitigates all but 1 hp of damage.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 3 minutes[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Special[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Air, Fire, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: Created as a party prank by the mirthful wizard, Roos Drobane.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Keen Edge
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A steaming, oily mixture that is indigo in color. It smells like milk, but tastes like peach.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]This potion turns the edges of hands and feet into sharp weapons that cause an additional 1d6 slashing damage on a successful attack.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 minute[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Offensive[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Fire, Life, Water[br]
articles.history: Developed by a monastic order to improve strike damage.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Lilliputians
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A swirling, watery liquid that is amethyst in color. It smells like walnuts and grass, but tastes like beer.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]This potion shrinks the imbiber to the size of a large ant while retaining enhanced strength - enough to easily move mechanisms weighing up to five pounds. The potion is popular with thieves and adventurers, granting them easy access to challenging areas. [p]The body starts tingling two minutes before it wears off as a warning signal to get somewhere safe before explosively growing back to full size. No damage is incurred as long as the imbiber is in a safe place before expanding back to full size. However, if the space is not big enough, it could result in tremendous damage and even death (at the DMs discretion.)[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 hour[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Spell[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]
articles.manufacturingProcess: [b]Crafter:[/b] Alchemist, Herbalist[br][b]Crafter Level:[/b] Level 11+[br][b]Crafting DC:[/b] 22[br][b]Crafting Time:[/b] 12.5 weeks[br][br]
articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Life, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: Created by a horologist to allow his assistants to work on the delicate repair of clock and clockwork mechanisms.[p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Lucky Seven
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A steaming, watery draught that is sapphire in color. It smells and tastes like peach.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Similar to Halfling's Luck, every initial roll of one on a d20 can be rerolled. The luck applies to all d20 rolls, including attack rolls, ability checks, and saving throws.[br][br]
articles.sidepanelcontenttop: [p][b]Application:[/b] Ingest[p][b]Duration:[/b] 1 minute[p][b]Effect Category:[/b] Mechanic[p][b]Source:[/b] Pru's Potions[hr]

articles.rawMaterials: [b]Base:[/b] Pure alcohol[br][b]Essencces:[/b] Earth, Life, Shadow, Water[br]
articles.history: Created by a cabal of seven dwarf wizards, this potion confers unusual luck on the imbiber. [p][/p]

articles.title: Potion of the Magnetic Gaze
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A watery draught that is amethyst with blue flashes. It smells like pine needles and grass, but tastes like herbs and lettuce.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]As an attack action, you can focus on an iron item, even a warrior in a set of plate armor, and hurl it up to 30’. If a creature is tossed, it takes 3d6 bludgeoning damage when it lands. [br][br]

It smells like oranges and bread, but tastes like grapes.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Allows the imbiber to unleash a whirlwind of radiant energy in a 30’ radius. The longer the imbiber can delay the release, the stronger the effect. [p]It works as follows: the imbiber makes a DC 12 Constitution check each round. If succeessfil, the potion does not automatically cause the radiant discharge (although the imbiber can choose to release its power at any time.) [p]The potion can cause 1d8 radiant damage immediately after drinking. The damage increases by 1d8 per round up to a maximum of 10d8. [p]Once the sphere of radiant energy is released, the potion’s effect ends. If the damage reaches its maximum level, it immediately releases.[br][br]

While in this state, the imbiber retains their full movement speed, can move through spaces as small as one inch, and occupy the same space as another, causing 1d6 necrotic damage per round while doing so. [p]The imbiber cannot physically interact with objects or communicate while in this state. [br][br]

It smells like coffee and vanilla, but tastes like oranges.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Turns the imbiber into a werewolf with all its powers and abilities for 1d6 minutes. The imbiber retains awareness during the transformation and acts in the same manner as the character would choose to act. [p]For a month after imbibing the potion, the imbiber’s hair grows at triple the normal rate, and their diet consists of only raw meat. [p]The stick is consumed after 1 round, and the smoke dissipates naturally over a period of up to 1 minute, depending on the wind conditions.[br][br]

articles.title: Tanglefoot Bag
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A sealed canvas bag that appears to be about 1' in diameter. When jostled, it appears to contain some form of goo.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]This fragile sack contains coiled strands of fibrous alchemical goo that expand and become sticky when exposed to air. [p]As an action, you can throw the bag up to 30 feet, causing an effect identical to the web spell (DC 12), filling a 5- foot cube, for 1 minute. [p]Large creatures have advantage on saving throws against the effects of the goo. Huge or larger creatures are unaffected. The bag does not function underwater.[br][br]

articles.title: The Verdant Harvester
articles.content:
This enchanted garden hoe, known as the Verdant Harvester, carries the essence of nature's magic. 

Once per day, when a character utilizes the hoe to cultivate a seed, an immediate burst of growth transforms the seed into a thriving plant. The verdant power within the hoe infuses the plant with vitality.

However, there is a caveat – the plant requires fertile soil to flourish. The magic of the hoe cannot overcome the challenges posed by rock or ice. Thus, for the Verdant Harvester to work its magic, a patch of soil must be present, ensuring the successful growth of the enchanted plant.

articles.title: The Voice
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A steaming fluid that is amethyst with gray flecks. It smells like pinapple, but tastes like lime.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]The imbiber gains the ability of ventriloquism to project their voice up to sixty feet away. The voice can be as quiet as a whisper or as loud as a deafening shout at close range. [p]It will distract a creature for one round when used at its highest volume if it fails a DC 14 Intelligence saving throw. In combat, failing the saving throw means the creature will have disadvantage on all attacks on its next turn due to the ringing in its ears. [p]Only one creature per round can be affected in this way.[br][br]

articles.title: Thunderstone
articles.content: [h2]Description[/h2]A dull, brittle crystal.[br][br][h2]Effects[/h2]Shatters upon striking a hard surface, creating a deafening bang audible out to 500 feet. [p]As an action, you can throw the thunderstone up to 30 feet. Each creature in a 15-foot radius of the point of impact must succeed on a DC 12 Constitution saving throw or be deafened for one minute.[br][br]
